_comment: Edit this file when you install the bullet-journal skill. These are the only knobs you need to set. Nothing personal or deployment-specific should live in SKILL.md. Set the active 'storage' block to one backend; '_storage_examples' shows other backends you could copy into 'storage'.

notion.notes: Columns: Date (date), Entry (text), Tags (multi-select). Use the Notion connector to create one row per entry.

file.notes: Append one line per entry: '- YYYY-MM-DD [tag1, tag2] entry text'. Single machine only.

storage.notes: Channel ID for your journal channel (e.g. #bullet-journal). The Slack connector resolves channels by ID, not by #name — find it in the channel's About tab or its URL after /archives/. Post one message per entry, e.g. 'YYYY-MM-DD — entry text [tag1, tag2]'. Write tags as plain bracketed text like [work, luck]; do NOT prefix tags with # or @, which Slack treats as channel and user references. For a running document instead of a feed, append to a Slack Canvas. The Claude Slack app must be a member of the channel. Writable from Slack on mobile, web, and desktop.
